title: cytisus supinus var. pygmaeus
intro: Cytisus supinus var. pygmaeus, also known as Pygmy Kidney Vetch, is a low-growing, woody perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. Its health benefits include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as promoting wound healing and reducing fever. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various health conditions and maintain overall well-being.
